comments.author: @romeowhiskey1146
comments.text: My father lived his life looking at ROAD ATLASES and where they would take my family. I wish that he was alive today. I know he would enjoy immensely the Where on Earth series. Kamchatka...what an amazing place that nobody knows anything about.

comments.author: @rikkimoore3914
comments.text: I needed a new documentary today!! One combining geography, vulcanology and history.

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Glad it hit that exact combination — Kamchatka doesn't come around often. Thank you for watching.

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: 봐주셔서 감사합니다 — 그 풍경 앞에서는 저희도 촬영하며 여러 번 숨을 죽였습니다.

comments.author: @trueunrealglobe
comments.text: I appreciate that this documentary explains the history and geography behind each location instead of relying only on beautiful drone shots to impress viewers.

comments.author: @marcelasantander7457
comments.text: Iceland  rides the northern extreme of the North Atlantic Ridge.  There are  new lands continuously emerging too; yet more gently and it is a beautiful landsccape.

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Good comparison — Iceland and Kamchatka sit on very different systems, but both keep proving the ground under them is nowhere near finished. Thanks for adding that.
